items.title: UK Education ‘Important’, Says British High Comm Amid NUMed Limbo
items.summary: Despite 103 NUMed graduates being stuck in a limbo due to the UK's new medical training law, the British High Commission says the UK will continue to promote UK education in Malaysia because education is an important pillar of the UK-Malaysia relationship. The post UK Education ‘Important’, Says British High Comm Amid NUMed Limbo appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Humane Cage-Free Farming Practices Growing In Malaysia: Advocates
items.summary: Malaysia is moving toward a more responsible and sustainable food production ecosystem. The rising adoption of cage-free systems reflects a growing understanding that animal welfare, sustainability, food safety, and business resilience are interconnected. The post Humane Cage-Free Farming Practices Growing In Malaysia: Advocates appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Cut Waste, Not Health: Tax Harm, Protect Malaysians — MyWatch
items.summary: At a time when public hospitals are overcrowded, primary care clinics are stretched, medicine costs are rising, and chronic disease burdens are worsening, cutting health spending is economically short-sighted and socially reckless. The post Cut Waste, Not Health: Tax Harm, Protect Malaysians — MyWatch appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: When Care Breaks Down At Home: Malaysia’s Hidden Crisis Of Dependent Ageing
items.summary: Malaysia’s true elder care workforce is found in the home, not institutions. These carers are the real frontline of dependent ageing. Yet policy still treats them as informal extensions of family duty, rather than essential components of the care system. The post When Care Breaks Down At Home: Malaysia’s Hidden Crisis Of Dependent Ageing appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Farmland Shrinks As Data Centres Expand, Raising Food Security Concerns
items.summary: KPKM warns that farmland is being converted for data centres and other industrial purposes, raising food security risks. Livestock land shrank 48% since 2017 due to development pressures. Data centre investments reached RM144 bil from 2021 to March 2025. The post Farmland Shrinks As Data Centres Expand, Raising Food Security Concerns appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Unfair Treatment At HTJS — Houseman
items.summary: Housemen have been stuck in their medical posting at HTJS for nearly a month now. It's taking a toll on the mental health of all housemen in this hospital because not only are we behind our other batchmates, other departments are severely lacking in HOs. The post Unfair Treatment At HTJS — Houseman appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: MOH Issuing New Circular On Junior Medical Officers’ Working Hours
items.summary: Dr Dzul says the Health DG will issue a new circular on junior medical officers' working hours, besides stressing compliance with flexi-work guidelines for housemen. Junior doctors in Malaysia work 65 to 85 hours weekly, far higher than the UK's 40 hours. The post MOH Issuing New Circular On Junior Medical Officers’ Working Hours appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Mudah Healthtech, Sihat XPress Kiosk To Accelerate Preventive Care
items.summary: Mudah Healthtech’s Sihat Xpress kiosk closes the health care gap between urban and rural areas, acting as a critical health care link. The post Mudah Healthtech, Sihat XPress Kiosk To Accelerate Preventive Care appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Sustainability Of Primary Care Supersedes Doctors’ Income: FPMPAM
items.summary: FPMPAM says MMA's survey about GP clinics appears to refer to clinic business revenue, rather than doctors' personal income, adding that the bigger issue is about the sustainability of Malaysia's community-based primary care system, not doctors' earnings. The post Sustainability Of Primary Care Supersedes Doctors’ Income: FPMPAM appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: ‘Godzilla’ El Niño May Slash Malaysia’s Rice Output
items.summary: Extreme weather projections could slash local rice production from ~40% contribution in key granary states to as low as 20%, says a KPKM official. Malaysia only produces 52% of its rice needs, leaving it exposed if exporting countries restrict supply. The post ‘Godzilla’ El Niño May Slash Malaysia’s Rice Output appeared first on CodeBlue .

items.title: Penang’s RM250m Taman Mengkuang water plant, set to reduce dependence on Sungai Dua supply, now 35pc complete
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, May 9 — The RM250 million Taman Mengkuang Water Treatment Plant (LRA) project undertaken by Perbad...
